content:
Case 8:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 78
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: Progressive fatigue, dyspnea on exertion, reduced appetite, and high fever with productive cough
- Vital signs: BP 86/54 mmHg, HR 128/min regular, T° 39.2 °C, RR 28/min, O₂ saturation 89% on room air.
Setting: Emergency Department / Acute Care (Initial Septic Presentation)
Capabilities: You are assessing an acutely ill patient with sepsis in an acute-care environment. You can perform urgent clinical evaluation, obtain baseline investigations, and arrange emergent non-invasive imaging appropriate for the acute situation. Invasive procedures and complex diagnostic evaluations usually follow once the patient is at least partially stabilized.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.

add antifungal if persistent fever", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Aggressive hemodynamic resuscitation with IV crystalloids and early norepinephrine for MAP \u226565; ICU admission and lactate-guided resuscitation", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Supportive hematology: transfuse platelets (goal \u226520\u201330\u00d710\u2079/L with sepsis/bleeding risk; solve.working_diagnosis: Neutropenic septic shock from bilateral pneumonia in the setting of suspected acute leukemia (likely AML) causing severe pancytopenia with circulating blasts
differentials: Myelodysplastic syndrome with excess blasts (MDS-EB) presenting with severe cytopenias and infection
Acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) in an older adult
Aplastic anemia (less likely given circulating blasts)
Disseminated intravascular coagulation from severe sepsis (vs leukemia-associated coagulopathy)

treatment_plan.action: Immediate sepsis bundle: high-flow oxygen, obtain blood cultures, and start empiric IV broad-spectrum antibiotics with antipseudomonal coverage (e.g., piperacillin-tazobactam or cefepime) ± vancomycin if MRSA risk; add antifungal if persistent fever
Aggressive hemodynamic resuscitation with IV crystalloids and early norepinephrine for MAP ≥65; ICU admission and lactate-guided resuscitation

Supportive hematology: transfuse platelets (goal ≥20–30×10⁹/L with sepsis/bleeding risk; higher if procedures) and PRBCs as needed; neutropenic precautions; consider G-CSF only if life-threatening infection after hematology input

Urgent diagnostic hematology workup once stabilized: peripheral blood smear review; send peripheral blood flow cytometry; arrange bone marrow aspirate/biopsy with cytogenetics/FISH and molecular testing; evaluate for APL promptly (PML-RARA)

Manage coagulopathy/possible DIC: repeat full DIC panel (fibrinogen, D-dimer), give vitamin K if appropriate, transfuse cryoprecipitate/FFP guided by fibrinogen/INR and bleeding; if APL suspected, start all-trans retinoic acid immediately while awaiting confirmation

content:
'peripheral blood smear findings (blast morphology, presence/absence of Auer rods) and pathologist comment' not available. Try 'cbc' instead.
This case does not define a separate peripheral blood smear/pathology report item. You can request the CBC (which includes the differential) or, for detailed morphologic and immunophenotypic assessment, request the bone_marrow item (bone marrow morphology and flow cytometry).
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone_marrow"}}

Bone Marrow:
Smears are of limited quality with low overall cellularity and very few spicules. Scattered blasts are present, accounting for approximately 10–15 % of nucleated cells. The blasts are medium to large in size with a high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, round to irregular nuclei, and finely dispersed chromatin. The cytoplasm is basophilic, occasionally containing azurophilic granules and rare vacuoles.
Dysplastic changes are observed in the residual myeloid series with hypogranulation, while erythropoiesis is markedly reduced, showing occasional megaloblastoid forms. Megakaryopoiesis is sparse, with small hypolobated megakaryocytes.
Flow cytometry demonstrated 23 % blasts expressing CD34⁺, CD117⁺, HLA-DR⁺, CD13⁺, CD33⁺, and CD38 (dim), with dim CD123 and weak MPO expression. There was no aberrant co-expression of CD7 or CD56

solve.working_diagnosis: High-risk myeloid neoplasm most consistent with myelodysplastic syndrome with excess blasts (MDS-EB2) vs hypocellular AML, presenting with profound pancytopenia and neutropenic septic shock from bilateral pneumonia
differentials: Acute myeloid leukemia (AML) with low blast count due to hypocellular/poor-quality aspirate or hemodilution (would be AML if blasts ≥20% on repeat/trephine)
Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm (if prior chemo/radiation exposure)

treatment_plan.action: Continue/adjust empiric therapy for febrile neutropenia and pneumonia in ICU (antipseudomonal beta-lactam; add MRSA and antifungal coverage based on cultures/clinical course) and source control; repeat cultures as indicated
Hemodynamic and respiratory support as needed (crystalloids/vasopressors; supplemental O2/ventilation) and monitor lactate/organ function

Transfusion and bleeding-risk management: PRBC transfusion to maintain adequate oxygen delivery; platelet transfusions (generally keep >10k, >20–30k with sepsis/fever, higher if bleeding/procedures) and correct coagulopathy guided by full DIC labs including fibrinogen and D-dimer

Complete definitive classification/prognosis: repeat bone marrow with core biopsy for cellularity and accurate blast %; send cytogenetics (karyotype/FISH) and myeloid NGS panel (e.g., TP53, ASXL1, RUNX1, FLT3, NPM1, IDH1/2) to distinguish MDS-EB2 vs AML and guide therapy

Once stabilized, initiate disease-directed therapy appropriate for age/fitness and genetics: hypomethylating agent (azacitidine or decitabine) plus venetoclax if AML/high-blast disease or very-high-risk MDS; consider HMA alone if frail; evaluate candidacy for allogeneic HSCT if performance status allows and disease risk is very high

Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

content:
Treatment History:
07/25/2018: Diagnosis of AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC, WHO 2016). 08/03/2018: Induction I with CPX-351: Liposomal daunorubicin 44 mg/m² (≈ 90 mg/day) + cytarabine 100 mg/m² (≈ 205 mg/day) on days 1, 3, and 5. Total dose per course: daunorubicin ≈ 270 mg, cytarabine ≈ 615 mg. Result: hypocellular bone marrow without detectable residual AML (MLFS)
10/21/2018 – Induction II with CPX-351: Same dosing as Induction I (days 1 and 3: daunorubicin ≈ 90 mg/day, cytarabine ≈ 205 mg/day). Result: CR with incomplete count recovery. 01/09/2019 – Allogeneic stem-cell transplantation (alloSCT): From a female HLA-identical unrelated donor after conditioning with fludarabine 30 mg/m² ≈ 61 mg/day i.v. (days -6 to -2), treosulfan 10 g/m² = 20.5 g/day i.v. (days –6 to –4), and rabbit-ATG 2.5 mg/kg (days -3 to 1). Donor/recipient CMV: negative/positive; both A Rh positive. Immunosuppression: cyclosporine 2.5 mg/kg BID (225 mg BID) starting day -3 and mycophenolate 1 g BID from day -1. 01/28/2019 (day +19): Bone marrow: complete molecular remission, donor chimerism 99.94%. 03/25/2020: EBV reactivation (152 copies/10⁵ cells), resolved spontaneously. 04/20/2020: Day +100 marrow: cytogenetic CR, donor chimerism 99.81%. 05/16/2020: Chimerism declined to 99.57% → DLI1 (1x10⁶ CD3⁺/kg) on 06/07/2020; DLI3 (5 x10⁶ CD3⁺/kg) on 07/07/2020; DLI3 (1x10⁷ CD3⁺/kg) on 08/10/2020 → donor chimerism 99.89%. 09/25/2020: DLI4 (5x10⁷ CD3⁺/kg). In October 2020, the patient developed acute gastrointestinal GvHD grade III. GvHD was treated with prednisolone 2 mg/kg (175 mg/day), followed by ruxolitinib 10 mg BID, etanercept 25 mg BIW x 4 doses, and vedolizumab 300 mg q14d → partial clinical resolution by 11/04/2020. 11/2020 – 08/2021: Serial marrows showed sustained CR, donor chimerism > 99.9%. 01/14/2023: Hematologic relapse: 14 % blasts by morphology, 27 % by FACS; mutations in ASXL1 (VAF 8 %), STAG2 (VAF 8 %), RAD21 (VAF 9 %), SRSF2 (VAF 8 %).
Chimerism: donor 98.6 %, recipient 1.4 %. 01/23/2023 – Start of azacitidine/venetoclax: Azacitidine 75 mg/m² s.c. days 1-7 (total = 1100 mg). Venetoclax ramp-up with posaconazole co-medication: 10 mg (d1), 20 mg (d2), 50 mg (d3), then 70 mg/day from d4 onward. 02/22/2023: Bone marrow CR (4 % blasts by morphology, 1 % by FACS), full molecular clearance of mutations.
CMV/EBV PCR negative. Continued azacitidine/venetoclax for 6 cycles, intervals gradually extended to 5 weeks, venetoclax duration shortened to 14 and then to 10 days per cycle due to cytopenia. 08/26/2023: Hospitalized for worsening Parkinsonian symptoms (bradykinesia, rigidity, tremor). No acute cause identified. Patient chose to stop azacitidine/venetoclax thereafter; neurological symptoms persisted. 01/12/2024: Bone marrow puncture: ongoing CR, donor chimerism 99.89 % (recipient 0.11 %).

considered only because of borderline blast percentages/limited sample)", "Infectious marrow suppression with leukoerythroblastic picture (unlikely given blast immunophenotype and prior relapse history)"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Treat septic shock and febrile neutropenia/pneumonia per ICU protocol: immediate broad-spectrum antipseudomonal IV antibiotics with escalation/de-escalation to cultures; add MRSA coverage if indicated; early mold-active antifungal if persistent fever/instability; supportive respiratory care and vasopressors as needed", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Transfusion and complication support: PRBC transfusion for symptomatic anemia/oxygen delivery; platelet transfusions for Plt 18\u00d710\u2079/L and sepsis (target per bleeding/procedures); manage renal/hepatic dysfunction and electrolyte abnormalities; avoid routine G-CSF unless directed by hematology in life-threatening infection", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Confirm and stage relapse once stabilized: repeat marrow with core biopsy for cellularity and accurate blast %; obtain chimerism, full cytogenetics/FISH and myeloid NGS (including FLT3, NPM1, IDH1/2, TP53) to identify actionable targets and to compare with 2023 relapse clone", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "If patient is candidate and infection controlled: restart low-intensity relapse therapy (azacitidine + venetoclax with careful dose adjustment for azole interactions and shortened venetoclax duration given prior cytopenias), as this previously achieved CR and molecular clearance; consider adding targeted agent if actionable mutation emerges (e.g., gilteritinib for FLT3, ivosidenib/enasidenib for IDH1/2)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Post-remission / relapse-control strategy: discuss palliative-intent vs disease-control intent given age 78, Parkinsonism, prior severe GI aGVHD after DLI; otherwise continue HMA/VEN as maintenance-style therapy or transition to best supportive care/hospice per goals", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Late post–alloSCT relapse of AML with myelodysplasia-related features (prior AML-MRC; now relapsed/refractory AML) presenting with neutropenic septic shock from bilateral pneumonia and severe pancytopenia, with low-level circulating blasts and marrow blasts ~10–15% (flow 23%) consistent with early morphologic relapse in a hypocellular/aspirate-limited sample
differentials: Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm / post-transplant therapy-related AML/MDS (less likely given established prior AML course but relevant category biologically)
MDS with excess blasts (post-transplant) rather than AML (would not explain prior AML history; considered only because of borderline blast percentages/limited sample)
Infectious marrow suppression with leukoerythroblastic picture (unlikely given blast immunophenotype and prior relapse history)

treatment_plan.action: Treat septic shock and febrile neutropenia/pneumonia per ICU protocol: immediate broad-spectrum antipseudomonal IV antibiotics with escalation/de-escalation to cultures; add MRSA coverage if indicated; early mold-active antifungal if persistent fever/instability; supportive respiratory care and vasopressors as needed

Transfusion and complication support: PRBC transfusion for symptomatic anemia/oxygen delivery; platelet transfusions for Plt 18×10⁹/L and sepsis (target per bleeding/procedures); manage renal/hepatic dysfunction and electrolyte abnormalities; avoid routine G-CSF unless directed by hematology in life-threatening infection

Confirm and stage relapse once stabilized: repeat marrow with core biopsy for cellularity and accurate blast %; obtain chimerism, full cytogenetics/FISH and myeloid NGS (including FLT3, NPM1, IDH1/2, TP53) to identify actionable targets and to compare with 2023 relapse clone

If patient is candidate and infection controlled: restart low-intensity relapse therapy (azacitidine + venetoclax with careful dose adjustment for azole interactions and shortened venetoclax duration given prior cytopenias), as this previously achieved CR and molecular clearance; consider adding targeted agent if actionable mutation emerges (e.g., gilteritinib for FLT3, ivosidenib/enasidenib for IDH1/2)

Post-remission / relapse-control strategy: discuss palliative-intent vs disease-control intent given age 78, Parkinsonism, prior severe GI aGVHD after DLI; if meaningful response and functional status acceptable, consider immunologic consolidation with carefully selected low-dose DLI or taper of immunosuppression only with extreme caution and transplant-team input due to prior grade III GI GVHD; otherwise continue HMA/VEN as maintenance-style therapy or transition to best supportive care/hospice per goals
